cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this problem step by step. So we have five people: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. Each starts with a specific colored ball. The question is about tracking the swaps and determining which ball Alice ends up with at the end. First, let me list out the initial assignments: - Alice: black ball - Bob: brown ball - Claire: blue ball - Dave: white ball - Eve: green ball Then there are a series of swaps. Let me go through each one carefully. First swap: Dave and Eve swap balls. So Dave had white, Eve had green. After swapping: - Dave now has green - Eve now has white Second swap: Bob and Claire swap balls. Bob had brown, Claire had blue. After swapping: - Bob now has blue - Claire now has brown Third swap: Claire and Alice swap balls. Claire now has brown (from the previous swap), Alice had black. After swapping: - Claire now has black - Alice now has brown Fourth swap: Dave and Eve swap balls again. Previously, after the first swap, Dave had green and Eve had white. Swapping again would revert them back, right? Because swapping twice between the same two people would return them to their original positions. So after this fourth swap: - Dave goes back to white - Eve goes back to green But wait, let me check that again. The first swap was Dave (white) and Eve (green) swapping. So after first swap: Dave has green, Eve has white. Then, fourth swap is again Dave and Eve swapping. So yes, swapping again would return them to original.
